### Post by shaloken on 2009-05-10
Hi,
yesterday I tried to install my driver Nvidia and i tried EnvyNG
after that, I reboot and it said that ubuntu will be executed with a low resolution graphics (800x600 @ 60Hz).
I change my Xorg.conf to be able to have a resolution of 1024 x 768 (i love this resolution)
but when I reboot (again) my web page where like i was with the 800 x 600. I think it's a problem with my Xorg.conf

This is my Xorg.conf:
```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder57)  Thu Jul 17 18:39:19 PDT 2008

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using
# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen      0  "Default Screen" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse" "CorePointer"
    InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "v4l"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "ca"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Synaptics Touchpad"
    Driver         "synaptics"
    Option         "SendCoreEvents" "true"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto-dev"
    Option         "HorizEdgeScroll" "0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Configured Monitor"
    VendorName     "Plug 'n' Play"
    ModelName      "Plug 'n' Play"
    Gamma           1
    ModeLine       "1024x768@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -hsync -vsync
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "monitor1"
    Gamma           1
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Configured Video Device"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA"
    BoardName      "NVIDIA GeForce 8 Series"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "device1"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA"
    BoardName      "NVIDIA GeForce 8 Series"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "Configured Video Device"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Virtual     640 480
        Depth       24
    Modes      "1024x768@60"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "screen1"
    Device         "device1"
    Monitor        "monitor1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection

```
If I made a mistake or I have lose something behind, tell me.

If anyone can help, it will be appreciated

Thanks

### Post by overdrank on 2009-05-10
Hi and welcome, I see one issue 
```
Section "Screen"
Identifier "Default Screen"
Device "Configured Video Device"
Monitor "Configured Monitor"
DefaultDepth 24
Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
SubSection "Display"
[COLOR="Red"]Virtual 640 480[/COLOR]
Depth 24
Modes "1024x768@60"
EndSubSection
EndSection
```
This may cause some issue. What version of Ubuntu are you using?
Have you tried setting your resolution using the nvidia settings?
```
gksu nvidia-settings
``` saving to your xorg.

---

### Post by shaloken on 2009-05-10
For your information, I use ubuntu 8.04
I tried your 2 solutions, but there is no change

---

### Post by overdrank on 2009-05-10
> **shaloken said:**
> For your information, I use ubuntu 8.04
I tried your 2 solutions, but there is no change

Ok so you are able to use the nvidia-settings?
If you are using Hardy 8.04 there is another tool, using the alt, F2 keys and enter the command ```
gksu displayconfig-gtk
``` and adjust.

---

### Post by shaloken on 2009-05-10
I tried your command and it worked, but the only choice I have in resolution is 800 x 600 or 480 x... i don't remember... but I don't have my resolution (1024 x 768) and if I change my screen and click detect it's a plug'n'play (because I'm on my laptop) and the resolution is the same

---

### Post by overdrank on 2009-05-10
> **shaloken said:**
> I tried your command and it worked, but the only choice I have in resolution is 800 x 600 or 480 x... i don't remember... but I don't have my resolution (1024 x 768) and if I change my screen and click detect it's a plug'n'play (because I'm on my laptop) and the resolution is the same

Ok so its a laptop, after opening the display config on the screens tab. If you select the resolution you are wanting from the list from model. Then click ok it will say it will apply once all users log off.
 You can log off or restart x with the ctrl, alt, backspace keys. When you log back in you should have that resolution and should also be available from the nvidia-settings. Please bare with me as I am working from memory and the screen shots [here]("https://wiki.ubuntu.com/DisplayConfigGTK")

---

### Post by shaloken on 2009-05-10
it's exactly like you say, 
my resolution came back to normal
Problem solve :)
thank you very much to have spend your time to my problem

---
